column2: e639440 Phase 9H: Sitemap/Taxonomy Recovery + Dog Food Publishing
WordPress REST API v2 + Live HTML + post-sitemap.xml
Use WP REST API to resave each post (content update trigger). Attempted multiple approaches: updateMeta, module toggle, content resave with force, draft-publish cycle.
column5: Posts remain unchanged; only internal RM table affected

column2: Set focus_keyword, robots meta, title, description via Rank Math updateMeta API for all 58 posts. All meta was set correctly but sitemap table still not populated.
column5: Meta is additive only; original content untouched

column2: Site owner opens each of the 52 missing posts in the WordPress block editor and clicks the Update button. This triggers Rank Math's save_post hook which registers the post in the internal sitemap table. Can be done ~1 min per post.
column5: No changes to content; only triggers internal hooks
column7: Yes - requires owner to manually open and save each post in WP admin

column2: Switch from Rank Math sitemap to WordPress native sitemap (/wp-sitemap.xml). WP native sitemap includes all published posts regardless of Rank Math state. Risk: loses Rank Math sitemap customizations.
column5: Re-enable Rank Math sitemap module to revert
column6: No - not recommended without owner approval

column2: Resubmit sitemap URL in Google Search Console after sitemap is fully recovered. Only effective after Options A-D fix the sitemap.
column6: No - prerequisite: sitemap must be fixed first
